User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Sumegha Handmades application. This entity stores basic profile information and does not include authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

phoneNumber.description: The user's primary phone number, potentially used for WhatsApp communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification or type for products, enabling organized browsing and filtering of handmade art items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category (e.g., 'Paintings', 'Sculptures', 'Jewelry').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category, typically for UI display.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single handmade art product available for sale in the 'Sumegha Handmades' store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the handmade product.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including materials, inspiration, and crafting process.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for inventory management.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs to high-resolution images of the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category the product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

stockQuantity.description: The number of units of this product currently in stock.

material.description: The primary material(s) used to create the product.

dimensions.description: Physical dimensions of the product (e.g., '10x15 cm', '5 inch diameter').

weight.description: The weight of the product, typically in grams or ounces.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if the product should be prominently displayed on the homepage or in featured sections.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

WishlistItem.title: WishlistItem
WishlistItem.description: Represents a single product that a user has added to their personal wishlist.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WishlistItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this wishlist item. (Relationship: User 1:N WishlistItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product added to the wishlist. (Relationship: Product 1:N WishlistItem)

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the wishlist.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, containing a collection of products they intend to purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated (e.g., item added/removed, quantity changed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product within a user's shopping cart, including its quantity and price at the time of addition.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product in the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

priceAtAddition.description: The price of the product at the moment it was added to the cart, useful for historical tracking.

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to this cart item.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's confirmed order, intended for WhatsApp checkout processing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Optional reference to the User who placed the order. Can be null for guest orders. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

customerName.description: The name of the customer placing the order, used for WhatsApp communication.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer for WhatsApp communication.

customerEmail.description: Optional email address of the customer for order confirmation or follow-up.

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending WhatsApp', 'WhatsApp Initiated', 'Confirmed', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

whatsappMessageSent.description: Flag indicating if the initial WhatsApp message for this order has been successfully initiated by the system.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a larger order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product included in the order item. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of this product within the order.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time the order was placed.

ProductView.title: ProductView
ProductView.description: Records when a user views a product, used for AI-enhanced product discovery and recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductView entity.

userId.description: Optional reference to the User who viewed the product. Can be null for anonymous views. (Relationship: User 1:N ProductView)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was viewed. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductView)

viewedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was viewed.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible only by the user themselves. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` for authorized access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Publicly viewable list of product categories. These documents are read-only for general users and managed by administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Publicly viewable catalog of handmade art products. These documents are read-only for general users and managed by administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Wishlist items belonging to a specific user. Authorization is path-based; the `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. This provides authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this wishlist item.

params.name: wishlistItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the wishlist item.

definition.description: The single shopping cart document for a user. The document ID can be a fixed string like 'current'. Authorization is path-based; the `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. This provides authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart.

definition.description: Items within a user's specific shopping cart. Authorization is path-based, implicitly inherited from the parent user's path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart item.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item.

definition.description: Stores confirmed orders. Can be user-specific (with `userId` field) or guest orders (with `userId` as null). Authenticated users can read their own orders by matching `request.auth.uid` to the `resource.data.userId` field. Administrators can access all orders.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Items within a specific order. To ensure authorization independence from the parent Order document, each OrderItem document **must include a denormalized 'orderOwnerUserId' field** (nullable string, copied from the parent Order's 'userId' field) for direct authorization checks without requiring a `get()` call to the parent Order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item.

definition.description: Records product views for analytics and recommendations. Includes an optional `userId` field for authenticated user views. Anonymous users can create views (with `userId` as null), and authenticated users can read their own views by matching `request.auth.uid` to the `resource.data.userId` field.

params.name: productViewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product view entry.

definition.description: This collection is used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` signifies that `request.auth.uid` corresponds to an administrator. The document itself can be empty or contain minimal metadata; its existence is key for authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID, who has administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for 'Sumegha Handmades' is designed to prioritize secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization rules, adhering to the core design principles.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design heavily leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data to eliminate `get()` calls in security rules. For example, `WishlistItem`, `Cart`, and `CartItem` entities are nested under `/users/{userId}/...`, where `userId` in the path directly matches `request.auth.uid`. This makes authorization atomic and easy to debug, as access is determined solely by the path and the authenticated user's ID, rather than requiring a lookup to a parent document's fields.

For `Order` and `ProductView` entities, which have an optional `userId` field (allowing for guest data or specific user ownership), authorization relies on matching `request.auth.uid` directly against the `resource.data.userId` field within the document itself. This avoids cross-document `get()` dependencies for the primary record.

To maintain Authorization Independence for `OrderItem`s, which are subcollections of `Order`s and whose access depends on the `userId` of the parent `Order`, the `userId` from the parent `Order` document must be **denormalized** into each `OrderItem` document (e.g., as `orderOwnerUserId`). This allows `OrderItem` rules to check `resource.data.orderOwnerUserId == request.auth.uid` directly, preventing a `get()` call to the parent `Order` document for authorization checks.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **Public Collections:** `/categories` and `/products` are top-level collections with a homogeneous security posture (public read-only). This enables efficient `list` operations for all users without requiring filtering in rules.
*   **User-Owned Collections:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/wishlistItems` and `/users/{userId}/cart/items` inherently support QAPs because the `userId` in the path (e.g., `match /users/{userId}/wishlistItems/{wishlistItemId} { allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId; }`) ensures that queries are automatically scoped to the current user's data. This prevents users from requesting data they are not authorized to access.
*   **Collections with `userId` field:** For `/orders` and `/productViews`, where `userId` is an internal field, rules will enforce query constraints. For instance, `allow list: if request.auth.uid != null && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;` for authenticated users. This means client-side queries MUST include `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` to succeed, ensuring security rules are not used as filters.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private User Data:** All personal user data (`WishlistItem`, `Cart`, `CartItem`) is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}/...`, enforcing path-based ownership (Private Data, Hierarchical Paths). The `User` profile itself lives at `/users/{userId}`.
*   **Public Catalog Data:** `Category` and `Product` are in their own top-level collections (`/categories`, `/products`) to signify their public, read-only nature.
*   **Transactional Data (`Order`, `OrderItem`):** Placed at the top level `/orders/{orderId}` to accommodate both authenticated and guest orders. Denormalization of the `Order`'s `userId` into `OrderItem`s ensures subcollection security without `get()` calls. This allows for clear distinctions between user-specific and guest-specific access patterns.
*   **Admin Roles (DBAC):** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{userId}` is used for database-backed access control. The existence of a document for a `userId` in this collection grants administrative privileges, allowing simple `exists()` checks in security rules without custom claims.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** Consistent naming (`userId`, `orderId`, `productId`) and clear path structures improve debuggability and maintainability of security rules and application code. All wildcards are descriptive, preventing ambiguity. `status` fields in entities like `Order` provide explicit state modeling.
